info.title: QA Utils API
info.description: REST API for QA Utils — exposes all tool functions as HTTP endpoints. Use the Swagger UI below to explore and test every endpoint interactively, or import the OpenAPI spec into your automation framework.

tags.name: Generators
tags.description: Generate UUIDs, passwords, NanoIDs, lorem ipsum, random strings

tags.name: Converters
tags.description: Base64, URL, hash, color, timestamp, base and case converters

tags.name: Analysers
tags.description: Text stats, email validation, JWT decoding, regex testing

tags.name: Formatters
tags.description: JSON formatting, HTML sanitization, SQL generation

tags.name: Agents
tags.description: Autonomous AI agent — run multi-step tasks and multi-agent orchestration pipelines with built-in tools

tags.name: GraphQL
tags.description: Proxy GraphQL queries, mutations, and introspection to any endpoint

post.summary: Count characters, words, sentences, lines and paragraphs

value.example:
Hello world. How are you?
I am fine.

post.summary: Validate an email address
Decode a JWT token (without signature verification)
post.description:
Decodes the header and payload of a JWT token.
**WARNING:** This does NOT verify the signature. Do not use decoded
claims to make security decisions in production code.

post.summary: Test a regular expression against text
Encode a string to Base64
Decode a Base64 string
Percent-encode a URL string
Decode a percent-encoded URL string
Parse a URL into its components
Generate a hash of a string
Convert a color between HEX, RGB, and HSL
Convert a Unix timestamp or ISO string to multiple formats

value.description: Unix timestamp (seconds or ms) or ISO 8601 string. Omit for current time.

post.summary: Convert a number between bases (2–36)
Convert text to a different case style
Parse and pretty-print JSON
Strip script tags and inline event handlers from HTML
post.description:
Removes `<script>` blocks and `on*` event attributes from HTML.
**Note:** For production sanitization use a dedicated library like DOMPurify.

post.summary: Generate a SQL statement

sql.example: SELECT id, name, email FROM users WHERE id = 1 ORDER BY name ASC LIMIT 10;

post.summary: Generate UUIDs (v4)
Generate a cryptographically secure password
Generate NanoID-style IDs
Generate Lorem Ipsum placeholder text
Generate a cryptographically random alphanumeric string
Convert Markdown to Confluence Wiki markup
post.description: Converts a Markdown document to Confluence Wiki markup syntax. Supports headings, bold, italic, strikethrough, inline code, fenced code blocks, tables, ordered/unordered lists, links, images, blockquotes, and horizontal rules.

markdown.example:
# Hello

Some **bold** and _italic_ text.

- Item 1
- Item 2

post.summary: Execute a GraphQL query or mutation
post.description: Proxies a GraphQL request to any endpoint. Supports queries, mutations, variables, operation names, and custom HTTP headers (e.g. Authorization).

variables.description: GraphQL variables as a JSON object

headers.description: Custom HTTP headers forwarded to the upstream endpoint

status.description: HTTP status code from the upstream GraphQL server

502.description: Upstream endpoint unreachable or returned non-JSON

post.summary: Introspect a GraphQL schema
post.description: Sends the standard GraphQL introspection query to the target endpoint and returns a parsed schema summary — types, fields, arguments, and enum values — with built-in meta-types filtered out.

post.summary: Generate a curl command for a GraphQL request
post.description: Returns the equivalent curl command string for the given GraphQL request without sending any actual request. Useful for sharing, debugging, or piping into a terminal.

get.summary: List all tools available to the AI agent
get.description: Returns the full set of built-in tools the agent can autonomously call when solving a task.

post.summary: Run a single autonomous agent task
post.description: Runs the AI agent observe-think-act loop until the task is complete or maxIterations is reached. The agent autonomously selects and calls built-in tools to complete the task.

task.example: Generate a UUID and base64-encode it

provider.description: AI provider to use for the agent

endpoint.description: Custom endpoint URL (e.g. Azure OpenAI or Ollama)

model.description: Model name — uses provider default if omitted

post.summary: Run a multi-agent orchestration pipeline
post.description:
Coordinates multiple specialized agents to solve a complex task.

- **sequential** — each agent's output feeds into the next agent as context (chain).
- **orchestrated** — a designated orchestrator agent breaks the task into sub-tasks, delegates them to worker agents in parallel, then synthesizes a final answer.

task.example: Analyse this codebase for security issues and write a remediation plan

orchestrator.description: Orchestrator agent config — required when mode is 'orchestrated'
